content.problemStatement:
You are given a K×KK \times KK×K matrix MMM. For each rrr and ccc (1≤r,c≤K1 \le r, c \le K1≤r,c≤K), let's denote the element in the rrr-th row and ccc-th column by Mr,cM_{r, c}Mr,c​. You are also given three integers NNN, aaa and ddd. Let's define a K×KK \times KK×K matrix
S=∑i=0NFa+i⋅d⋅Mi .S = \sum_{i=0}^N F_{a + i \cdot d} \cdot M^i \,.S=∑i=0N​Fa+i⋅d​⋅Mi.
Here, M0M^0M0 is the identity matrix and for any non-negative integer iii, FiF_iFi​ is the iii-th Fibonacci number ― formally, F0=0F_0 = 0F0​=0, F1=1F_1 = 1F1​=1 and Fi=Fi−1+Fi−2F_i = F_{i-1} + F_{i-2}Fi​=Fi−1​+Fi−2​ for i>1i \gt 1i>1.

Let's denote elements of the matrix SSS in the same way as for MMM. For each valid rrr and ccc, compute Sr,cS_{r, c}Sr,c​ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 1 ≤ K ≤ 100 ( ΣK over all test cases ≤ 100 )
0 ≤ N ≤ 10^{1000} (N is given as a decimal string)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains four space‑separated integers K, a, d and N. Then K lines follow, each containing K integers M_{i,1} … M_{i,K} (0 ≤ M_{i,j} < 998244353).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output K lines. The i‑th line contains K space‑separated integers S_{i,1} … S_{i,K}, where S = Σ_{i=0}^{N} F_{a+i·d}·M^{i} and all values are taken modulo 998244353.

solution.approach: The term F_{a+i·d} can be expressed with the 2×2 Fibonacci Q‑matrix Q = [[1,1],[1,0]]: F_n = (Q^n)[0][1]. Let A = Q^a and D = Q^d. Then F_{a+i·d} = (A·D^i)[0][1]. The required sum is S = Σ_{i=0}^{N} (e0^T·A·D^i·e1)·M^i, where e0 = [1,0]^T and e1 = [0,1]^T. We compute three sequences for a length L = N+1 (the number of terms):   • P(L) = M^L,   • Q(L) = D^L,   • S(L) = Σ_{i=0}^{L-1} (e0^T·A·D^i·e1)·M^i.   Using binary exponentiation on the binary representation of L we can combine two halves in O(log L) steps:   If we already know (S, P, Q) for length len, then for length 2·len:   • S(2·len) = S(len) + P(len)·(e0^T·A·Q(len)·e1)·S(len)   • P(2·len) = P(len)·P(len)   • Q(2·len) = Q(len)·Q(len)   The scalar (e0^T·A·Q(len)·e1) is obtained by multiplying the 2×2 matrices A and Q(len) and taking element (0,1). When processing a bit equal to 1 we additionally append one more term using the current powers. All matrix multiplications are performed modulo MOD.   Because N can have up to 1000 decimal digits we treat it as a big integer string and repeatedly divide by 2 to obtain its binary representation (O(|N|·log N) ≤ 10^4 operations, easily fast).   The overall complexity per test case is O(K^3·log N) (K ≤ 100, log N ≤ 3500), well within limits. Memory usage is O(K^2).
solution.timeComplexity: O(K^3 · log N) per test case
